body_txt: Characters appear on a real or fictitious Game Show , but typically they won't win any significant amount of money. Often, two or more characters will be pitted against each other, despite the unlikelihood of two people who know each other being competitors on a real game show. If a group of people love a game show with one holdout pointing out the show's silliness, chances are the holdout will be the one who becomes a contestant and/or gets obsessed . Compare Jeopardy! Intelligence Test , where the characters reveal their knowledge or lack thereof by their reactions to watching a game show. Talk Show Appearance is this tropes talk show equivalent. Typically, using a real game show will involve the genuine set, props, and staff.
